title: NOC 43201 – Correctional Service Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Correctional Service Officers under NOC 43201 are responsible for maintaining safety, security, and order within correctional institutions. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation involves supervising offenders, enforcing institutional regulations, and supporting rehabilitation and reintegration programs. These professionals work in federal, provincial, and territorial correctional facilities across Canada.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group432 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionMinor Group4320 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionNOC Code43201

duties: Observe offender conduct to prevent disturbances and escapes
Supervise offenders during work assignments and recreation
Patrol assigned areas and report security concerns
Conduct security checks and visitor screenings
Escort detainees during transfers or temporary leaves
Prepare admission, transfer, and release documentation
Collaborate with psychologists, counsellors, and parole officers
Assist in developing and monitoring offender correctional plans
employment_requirements: (Required in New Brunswick, Quebec, Alberta, and Prince Edward Island)
Completion of federal Correctional Service of Canada training for federal institutions
Basic training course required for provincial/territorial institutions
Physical fitness and agility standards must be met
First aid and CPR certification usually required
Supervisory roles require prior experience as a correctional officer
Background checks and psychological assessments are commonly required.
salary_prospects: Correctional Service Officers typically earn between:
$48,100 – $82,700 per year,depending on province, experience, and institutional level.
Northern territories often offer higher wages.
job_outlook: Focus on inmate rehabilitation and reintegration
labour_market_demand: Correctional Service Officers are employed by:
Federal correctional institutions (Correctional Service of Canada)
Expansion of rehabilitation and reintegration programs
Opportunities to work in rehabilitation programs
Correctional services remain an essential public protection occupation throughout Canada.
pathways: Correctional Service Officers fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
If you are planning to work in Canada’s correctional services sector, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your pathway to Canada PR.
